result:
Unit D complete. Clean-room crate `cli-clean` reproduces the tamarin-prover CLI text surface byte-for-byte, derived entirely from black-box oracle probing (no tamarin-rs source read). 48 oracle calls logged in QUERIES.log; model in BEHAVIOR.md; report narrative in this retained transcript (REPORT.md write blocked by harness).
Deliverables at /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/console/workspace/cli-clean/ : parse(argv)->Result<Command,CliError>, render_help, render_version, and framing (frame_processed/frame_parse_only/render_summary/progress_line) plus errors + per-mode flag tables.
Covered: global+per-mode --help (modes interactive/variants/test), --version/-V (interleaved maude-check + banner, dynamic slots), error taxonomy (Unknown flag / Unhandled argument / Ambiguous mode / no-input+help / directory-not-exist+help / openFile does-not-exist|permission-denied|is-a-directory / CallStack app errors), and batch framing (maude preamble, 5 [Theory X] progress lines, opaque theory payload, 78-= summary block with verified|falsified|analysis-incomplete and WARNING lines).
Flag semantics: mode by unique prefix; leading flag or non-mode token stays batch (token=FILE); long value via =, short via attachment, following token never consumed; --version only in default mode.
Evidence: cargo test 22/22 pass vs independent captures; compiled binary 12/12 byte-match vs stored captures; live diff vs oracle 5/5 identical.
Limitations documented: true zero-arg unobservable; falsified string inferred; warning-vs-lemma summary ordering unobserved; output-json/dot missing-value not error under parse-only.
No forbidden path accessed; examples symlink resolves to the wellformedness cluster (observation inputs), oracle binary invoked only via hs_oracle.sh.

Unit E complete. All deliverables in `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/macros/workspace/`; acceptance suite (14 Rust tests + 6 oracle byte-parity fixtures) passes ALL PASS.

Clean-room Unit E (.spthy macro expansion) delivered; no tamarin-rs source or tamarin-prover internals were read — every claim traces to a logged oracle observation (workspace/QUERIES.log, 31 probes).
Core: workspace/macro-clean/src/lib.rs — pub fn expand(&Theory)->Theory inlines every macro call at all use sites (rules, lemmas, restrictions, predicates, diff terms, Sapic processes) and drops the macros: block; src/ast.rs vendors the interface type surface; src/tests.rs has 14 passing direct-expansion checks.
Semantics observed and implemented: simultaneous (capture-avoiding) substitution; formal-vs-body matching by FULL identity incl. sort (~x/$x do not match untagged x); transitive expansion over a DAG (forward/self/mutual refs are parse errors, so it always terminates); nullary macros with free vars captured by scope; arity enforced by the parser (>=2 exact, arity-1 packs extra args into a pair) so AST calls always match arity; duplicate/builtin-collision names rejected; macros preserved in modulo-E display but expanded in modulo-AC/expanded-formula/guarded-formula/diff/process views; --parse-only does no expansion.
Byte-parity method: each macro theory is paired with a hand-inlined equivalent (workspace/fixtures/); workspace/byteparity.sh runs the oracle on both and, after stripping the only three cosmetic scaffolding differences, confirms the expanded content is byte-identical.
Fixtures verified: capture, chain3, over_u3, sortmatch, issue777, MacroInLemmasAndRestrictions — all OK.
Docs/evidence: workspace/BEHAVIOR.md (full semantics table), workspace/QUERIES.log (oracle log), workspace/captures/ (raw oracle outputs), workspace/REPORT.md (report).
Reproduce: bash /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/macros/workspace/run_tests.sh.
Scope notes: xor example excluded from strict byte-parity (oracle AC-abstracts h(x⊕y⊕z) into a variant table); let-inlining, diff-projection, AC-variant computation and Sapic translation are separate passes left intact with inner macros expanded; equations expanded uniformly as a no-op safeguard.
Build is warning-free (cargo build/test clean on rustc 1.96.1).

All deliverables complete. Returning the summary.

Unit G (Message Derivation Checks) clean-room implementation done. Crate: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/derivcheck/workspace/derivcheck-clean/ (src/lib.rs, src/ast.rs, Cargo.toml). Clean build, 15/15 stubbed-callback tests pass.
Architecture: a caller-supplied DerivabilitySolver trait (fn check(&DerivProbe) -> Derivability{Derivable,NotDerivable,TimedOut}) makes the "can intruder derive X from Y" decision; my crate owns probe construction, decision logic, and byte-exact text — the solver is never reimplemented.
Behavior observed from oracle only: topic "Message Derivation Checks" (25-char '=' underline), emitted after Subterm Convergence; per-rule block "Rule <name>: \nFailed to derive Variable(s): <vars>" (trailing space after colon) with a fixed 2-space-indent intro; rules in theory order; [no_derivcheck] and IntrRule excluded; derivable rules omitted.
Variables sorted by (sort-rank Fresh<Msg, name lexicographic, index numeric); public vars never appear; duplicates collapse; rendered ~fresh/$pub/plain-msg with .idx for nonzero index. --derivcheck-timeout=0 deactivates (empty report); '=' is required (-d 1 mis-parses).
Byte-parity: my two-rule expected string is cmp-identical (297 bytes) to the real POIDC_CMB capture; render_derivation_report reproduces the oracle block exactly.
Residual (documented): a real per-rule timeout could not be forced (the check is fast; xor slowness is later precomputation), so TimedOut handling defaults to fail-open SuppressRule, with SkipVariable as a one-arm alternative.
Deliverables: /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/derivcheck/workspace/BEHAVIOR.md, /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/derivcheck/workspace/QUERIES.log, /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/derivcheck/workspace/REPORT.md.
Compliance: no path under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ was read; only oracle/hs_oracle.sh was executed and its captured stdout observed; input model taken from ../wellformedness/interface/ast_types.rs; sibling wf-clean source was not opened.

All 55 tests pass, clippy clean. Round 3 complete. Final summary:

Round 3 modeled the interactive-UI state machine as src/dispatch.rs, a Server<T: ProverOps> that owns a version map + monotonic counter and makes every web-layer decision while the prover trait supplies opaque fragments and mutations.
Version model: proof ops (main/method, autoprove) allocate a fresh index = max+1 and keep old versions resolvable; structural edits (POST edit/add/delete) mutate in place at the same index; next/prev and views never bump — all confirmed live ([L8]-[L12]).
autoprove decoded fully: autoprove/{strategy in idfs,characterize}/{bound 0=unbounded,5=bounded}/{allSol False,True}/proof/{lemma}[/path] — the a/b/all/characterization matrix from the keyboard-help ([Q031]); each answers 200 + JSON {redirect} to overview/proof at the new version.
Proof-step apply is main/method/{lemma}/{n}[/path] (number before path); delete=303 to overview/help; edit/add success=303 to overview/edit|add, failure=200 re-rendered form; proof paths are raw [A-Za-z0-9_] segments (root _), no percent-encoding.
Blocker (b) SOLVED: edit-form textarea rows = (#newlines in raw lemma text) + 2 (9/11/7/10 -> 11/13/9/12), replacing the round-1 hardcoded rows="8"; full edit-form envelope now byte-parity for two lemmas.
Blocker (a) NEGATIVE (environment-limited): with the server bound to all interfaces and the peer confirmed as 212.100.173.110 in the access log, every route is byte-identical loopback vs non-loopback; Host/X-Forwarded-For have no effect — the 1.13.0 build treats own-interface IPs as local, so a foreign peer is unobservable on one host; left as a parameterized origin_local hook, not fabricated.
Corrected an earlier note: the 6 status-0 autoprove entries carry the crawler's own "REQUEST_ERROR: TimeoutError" string, not a server response family.
Tests: cargo test = 55 passing (21 unit + 15 new dispatch + 19 parity), cargo clippy --tests --examples clean; fixtures r3_* are captured oracle output.
Protocol: derived only from oracle captures, the sanctioned binary (executed with permitted extra flags --help/--interface=*4), and sibling example .spthy files used as server inputs; no file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ was read; BEHAVIOR.md §13 and QUERIES.log [Q029]-[L16] updated; REPORT3.md written.
Key paths: workspace/web-clean/src/dispatch.rs, src/route.rs, src/forms.rs, tests/dispatch.rs, tests/fixtures/r3_*, workspace/BEHAVIOR.md, workspace/QUERIES.log, workspace/REPORT3.md.

Round 3 (GENERATION) complete. 44 tests green; servers stopped; non-access maintained. REPORT3.md was blocked by the harness — its content is folded into BEHAVIOR.md §6–§8. Ten-line summary:

graphdot Round 3 extended graph-clean from serialization to GENERATION over its own input model; every claim traces to the DOT corpus (12022), paired json/dot manifests, or live black-box probes (NAXOS_eCK, NSLPK3 via a corrected --port= wrapper). Item 3 (node-id/port allocation): one global counter, ports-before-node per record in emission order — verified 12022/12022 in Python and again in Rust via the crate's NodeIdAllocator. Item 1 (record-cell rendering): fact/function/tuple spacing, info-cell shape, empty-group drop (100% of 160409 records carry an info cell), record escaping, and the wrap FORMAT (continuation indent = first-element column, verified across 188192 cells, `\l` separators) all implemented and byte-tested against an observed wrapped cell; the break DECISION (line 5..87, not flat-width) is a documented pretty-printer gap. Item 2 (shapes): corpus has only record/ellipse/plain; live probing revealed invtrapezium `(#var, idx)` open-target nodes (added to model/serializer with fixtures) while plain trapezium was never observed. Item 4 (content): characterized the system→graph mapping (rule→record groups, !KU/action/compressed ellipses, invtrapezium, 11 fixed edge styles) and implemented `generate` over a `System` model that reproduces a live source-case graph byte-exact end-to-end. Item 5 (simplification): mined the UI JS to find the cookie-driven graph-URL params (simplification=N, uncompact/uncompress at L0, unabbreviate, no-auto-sources, clustering) and confirmed by live diffs — compression hides Fresh/intruder nodes, unabbreviate inlines terms; L1/L2/L3 were byte-identical on every probed graph (distinction is a gap) and compress/compact content is a solver gap; modeled in `Options`. New modules: alloc, render, generate, options; new fixtures nsl_invtrap/invtrap_compressed/invtrap_raw; shared test parser extracted to parse_util. `cargo test` = 44 green (deps: std + serde only); opt-in corpus gates pass 12022/12022 for both round-trip and allocator. Honest residuals remain solver/pretty-printer internals: wrap decision, L1/L2/L3 split, compression content, color hashes, and the unobserved trapezium. BEHAVIOR.md (§3d/§3e/§3f/§6/§7/§8) and QUERIES.log (Session 3) updated; no tamarin-rs source was read — only the black-box binary executed and captured OUTPUT read.

The corpus run continues in the background (17/49 processed; heavy diff/sapic theories consume their full budget). The completion waiter `bf7muwi5c` will notify me when it finishes. Let me wait for that, then run the final full validation and finalize the tally.

I'll pause here until the corpus completes.

Round 3 (Unit C) complete. Tests 37→53, all green; 12 new r3_ fixtures each re-verified byte-for-byte against the live oracle. No file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ was read; every behavior traces to an oracle probe. BEHAVIOR.md + QUERIES.log updated. REPORT3.md was blocked by the harness (report files must be returned as text), so its content is folded into BEHAVIOR.md's new "Round 3 closed and residual" section. 10-line summary follows:

Round 3 (Unit C) — wellformedness surface completion. Baseline 37 → 53 tests, all passing; 12 new byte-parity fixtures, each diff-verified against the live oracle.
1. Formula terms FULL coverage: rewrote to a term-based check; new `formula_terms_reducible(thy,&reducible)` / `check_theory_with_reducible` consume a caller-supplied reducible-symbol set; renders `Bound N` (de Bruijn, 0=innermost, temporals included), `Free x`, `f(a,b)` (no space), `pair(a,pair(b,c))`; free vars inside terms report the whole term; no dedup, source order, temporal-first in actions; fillSep list wrap at col 69; multi-lemma join `\n  \n`.
2. Formula guardedness: fixed nested-subformula selection (report the failing quantifier subtree); discovered+implemented the second reason "universal quantifier without toplevel implication" (∀ body must be a top-level implication whose antecedent guards all vars); added a HughesPJ-style multi-line formula printer (page width ~72, quantifier hangs at base+2, binary op right-operand hangs at its `(` column) — implies-break and And-break byte-exact; narrow formulas unchanged.
3. Fact capitalization issues: new topic (before Fact arity), Send vs SEND, laid out like arity but lists every occurrence (no dedup), item `Rule 'R', capitalization "Send"`.
4. Closed AST-derivable gaps: lemma-sourced fact arity/multiplicity now gathered (raw Haskell `Fact {factTag = ProtoFact …, factTerms = [Bound N…]}` render; restrictions excluded); fresh-const list wrapping (fillSep 69); multi-group public-names separator `\n  \n`.
Residual (documented, not closed): rule-printer wide fact-list wrapping (item 4b) — needs a dedicated rule-layout engine and is entangled with the out-of-scope Maude reprint; single-line rule printer stays byte-exact for short rules.
Method: pure black-box probing of oracle/wf_oracle.sh (probes in workspace/scratchpad/probes); no tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover source or memory used.
Key files: src/checks.rs (Formula-terms rewrite, fact_capitalization, unified fact-use gather, two-mode guardedness), src/formula.rs (pp_formula_wrapped engine), src/lib.rs (new entry points + Fact-cap in pipeline/anchors), tests/checks_tests.rs (+16), tests/fixtures/r3_*.txt (12).
Deliverables updated: workspace/BEHAVIOR.md and workspace/QUERIES.log; REPORT3.md content included above (file write blocked by harness).
